name: Starship Mars Crew
first: First proposed crewed return mission to Mars (SpaceX architecture)
description: SpaceX's long-term crewed Mars architecture as outlined in Elon Musk's 2016, 2017, and 2024 IAC presentations. A Starship lifts off from Earth fully fuelled in low-Earth orbit via a fleet of refilling tanker flights, performs trans-Mars injection, and lands a crew of roughly 10 with cargo and ISRU equipment on the Martian surface. Return-leg propellant (methane + liquid oxygen) is manufactured on Mars from atmospheric CO₂ and subsurface water ice via the Sabatier process, enabling the same Starship to lift off again, perform trans-Earth injection, and return the crew. Round-trip duration depends on the launch window and surface stay; the illustrative timeline shown here is ~2.5 years door-to-door (213 days outbound, ~500-day surface stay, return on the next favourable window). The architecture is conceptual — date and parameters subject to substantial revision pending Starship maturation and ISRU validation.

events.label: STARSHIP LAUNCH
events.note: Crewed Starship lifts off from Boca Chica or Cape Canaveral.

events.label: ORBITAL REFILLING
events.note: Tanker fleet rendezvous and propellant transfer in LEO across multiple flights.

events.label: TMI BURN
events.note: Trans-Mars injection from fully-fuelled LEO state.

events.label: MARS LANDING
events.note: Powered descent to Mars surface — no parachutes, no airbags, no sky-crane.

events.label: RETURN PROP COMPLETE
events.note: ISRU plant has produced sufficient methane + LOX for return-trip propellant.

events.label: TEI BURN
events.note: Trans-Earth injection — Starship lifts off Mars surface and burns for Earth-return trajectory.
